page: Google Cloud Platform n4d-highmem-32 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs based on the x86_64 architecture. The server is configured with 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. This high resource density is designed to support memory-intensive workloads without requiring excessive compute scaling.

This instance type does not include local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met through external network-attached block storage. Additionally, the configuration includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses and does not feature any GPU accelerators. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing performance, free from the resource contention typical of shared-core environments.

Given the 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the n4d-highmem-32 is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads where memory capacity is the primary bottleneck rather than raw compute power. It is suited for applications such as medium-sized databases, in-memory caching layers, enterprise application servers, and memory-bound data processing tasks.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4d-highmem-32 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs on the x86_64 architecture and 256.0 GB of RAM. With a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU, this instance is designed for memory-intensive workloads. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, requiring external block storage for data persistence. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures stable, predictable performance. This configuration is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-bound applications, making it suitable for databases, in-memory caching, and enterprise application hosting.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring a high memory-to-core ratio and dedicated processing resources for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs for predictable compute performance.
256.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core.
No local storage included, requiring external network-attached storage.
Zero GPU accelerators and zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.
Optimized for databases, in-memory caching, and memory-bound enterprise applications.
